scope: Running `sworn models` lists, per linked provider, the models actually available on the user's account — grouped by the resolution prefix the user would type — with capability annotations sourced only from wire-reported metadata (tools: yes/no/unknown), so explicit-prefix resolution (S05) ships with its discoverability counterpart and unknown never counts as capable.

reachability.evidence: TestModelsCommand (cmd/sworn/models_test.go) drives the registered `models` command's Run function end to end through command.Lookup("models").Run(nil) — the exact integration point main.dispatch resolves at runtime (Rule 1) — not a leaf internal/model/catalog.go unit test. It configures all 7 providers via env vars, points the 6 HTTP-based providers at a combined fixture server through a host-routing test Transport (modelsHTTPClient seam in cmd/sworn/models.go, redirecting requests aimed at the 6 real production hosts to the fixture while passing Ollama's already-local fixture host through unmodified), and asserts the rendered stdout carries every provider's grouped-by-prefix block with the correct wire-sourced tools annotation (AC-01, AC-02). TestModelsCommand_ProviderFilter, _AllFailedExitsNonZero, _PartialFailureExitsZero, and _UnknownProviderFlag exercise the same cmdModels entrypoint for AC-01's --provider filter and AC-03's per-provider isolation + exit-code rule. Manual smoke step (not run this session — no live provider credentials in this environment): `sworn models --provider mistral` against a real MISTRAL_API_KEY would print the account's actual Mistral models with real capability annotations.

delivered.item: AC-01: `sworn models [--provider <prefix>]` queries the models/list endpoint of every provider with credentials configured (no-dispatch availability — D1), prints models grouped by resolution prefix, and --provider filters to one.

AC-02: capability annotations sourced exclusively from wire-reported metadata — OpenRouter supported_parameters, Mistral capabilities.function_calling, Ollama /api/show capabilities, with fail-closed Unknown for bare-ID-list providers (OpenAI/Groq/Anthropic) and for Google (D4 — supportedGenerationMethods carries no explicit tool-support signal). No model-ID heuristics anywhere.

AC-03: a provider's models/list failure (auth, network, non-2xx) prints a per-provider error naming the provider, continues listing the rest, and the process exits non-zero only when every attempted provider failed.

AC-04: no completion/dispatch/probe calls anywhere — every request targets a models/list-shaped endpoint only (Ollama's two paths, /api/tags and /api/show, are the sole two-path exception, D2), proven by a per-provider allowlisted transport recorder that fails the test on any other path; go build ./... and go test ./cmd/sworn/... ./internal/model/... pass.
delivered.evidence: internal/model/catalog_test.go:TestListCatalog_NoDispatchPaths; go build ./... and go test ./cmd/sworn/... ./internal/model/... (test_results above)

delivered.item: D1 (Coach-ratified, captain-proceed.md pin 2): catalog.go runs its own no-dispatch credential-presence check against model.ProviderConfig for all 7 providers uniformly, not a partial reuse of internal/driver/registry.Drivers() (which structurally excludes Google/Ollama by its own header comment).
delivered.evidence: internal/model/catalog.go:catalogProviderDefs (configured funcs); design.md "Design gap the spec text doesn't resolve" section

delivered.item: D2/D3/D4 design decisions confirmed as designed (captain-proceed.md pin 2): Ollama's per-model /api/show N+1 call, Ollama always attempted regardless of credentials, Google's tools annotation unconditionally Unknown.
delivered.evidence: internal/model/catalog.go:listOllamaModels, ollamaModelTools, catalogProviderDefs (ollama configured func), listGoogleModels; status.json:design_decisions

delivered.item: Coach pin disposition 1 (captain-proceed.md): design.md's HTTP-client-convention section corrected — anthropic.go uses anthropic-sdk-go (an ADR-0007 exception), not stdlib net/http like oai.go/ollama.go.
delivered.evidence: design.md "Grounding" section (corrected in the start-implementation commit 0c4d4e6)

delivered.item: Coach pin disposition 3 (captain-proceed.md): the pricing-column follow-on's Rule 2 deferral now carries all three legs — tracking issue sworn#92.
Coach pin disposition 4 (captain-proceed.md): cmd/sworn/main.go correctly untouched (self-registration precedent) — recorded as a touchpoint divergence.
Coach pin disposition 5 (captain-proceed.md): the reachability artefact names TestModelsCommand explicitly and describes its fixture-driven end-to-end path through cmdModels.

not_delivered: Pricing display (OpenRouter's wire-honest `pricing` block) — NOT implemented (Rule 2: why = no AC requires it, and PriceForModel is keyed by the registry's fully-resolved provider/model ID, not catalog's raw per-provider wire IDs, so wiring it in would need its own normalisation pass — adding an unasked capability-shaped surface risks scope creep; tracking = sworn#92, filed and confirmed during design review per captain-proceed.md pin 3; acknowledgement = Coach-ratified proceed, captain-proceed.md pin 3, 2026-07-11). Left out of catalog.go entirely.

Active capability probing via paid test calls, registry/resolution changes (S05's territory), and catalog caching/auto-refresh — all out of scope per spec.json's own out_of_scope list; no work attempted against any of them this session.
divergence: cmd/sworn/main.go is untouched despite being named in spec.json's touchpoints list. main.go's own header states 'Adding a new CLI command never edits this file' (self-registration precedent, T15-owned command registry) — models.go self-registers via init(), matching every other verb in cmd/sworn. Flagged in design.md before code, accepted at design review with no action required (captain-proceed.md pin 4).

design.md's HTTP-client-convention section was corrected during this implementation session per Coach pin disposition 1 (captain-proceed.md): the design's grounding text had cited anthropic.go as using stdlib net/http like oai.go/ollama.go, when it in fact uses anthropic-sdk-go (an ADR-0007-documented exception, alongside google.go's genai SDK). The correction is documentation-only — catalog.go itself was always designed and implemented as stdlib-only (models.list needs no SDK dispatch machinery), so no code changed as a result.

TestListCatalog_OllamaAlwaysAttempted's fixture strategy diverges from design.md's literal test-plan wording ('empty ProviderConfig still yields an Ollama CatalogResult (error, since no fixture server is at the default host in test)'). This dev/CI host was found during implementation to run a real local Ollama daemon on the env-default host (localhost:11434, confirmed via `curl` returning a real model list) — asserting on the env-default host's failure would have made the test environment-dependent and flaky. The test instead points OllamaHost at an explicit closed local port (http://127.0.0.1:1) for a deterministic dial failure, preserving the behaviour under test (Ollama is attempted even with zero configured credentials) without depending on ambient machine state. Documented inline in catalog_test.go's TestListCatalog_OllamaAlwaysAttempted doc comment.

Ollama's /api/show call is implemented as POST with a JSON {"name": <model>} body (Ollama's actual documented API contract), not the literal 'GET {host}/api/show' design.md's per-provider table prose states. This is a faithful realisation of D2 (call /api/show per model for the capabilities wire signal) at the correct HTTP-verb level for a working implementation against the real Ollama API — the design's table prose was imprecise on this one detail, not a design-decision change; no re-review was sought as this is a mechanical correctness fix, not a Type-1 or Type-2 design choice.

The 'sworn lint coverage' and 'sworn llm-check -type ac-satisfaction' gates were not run to completion this session: lint coverage fails closed with a false-negative 'read spec.md: no such file' on this spec-v1 (spec.json, not spec.md) slice — a known project hazard (feedback_releaseverify_specmd_false_fail memory note) not specific to this slice. llm-check requires a configured model (--model or $SWORN_MODEL); this implementer environment has zero provider API keys configured, so no LLM-backed gate (ac-satisfaction, security-review) could be dispatched. Both are environmental/tooling limitations of this session, not a scope cut — declared here per Rule 2 rather than silently skipped or contorted around.

'sworn verify' (deterministic first-pass, the proof-bundle verification gate) required a --verifier-model with a resolvable provider key even though it is never dispatched on the non-agentic path — this dev machine's local ~/.config/sworn/config.json already names a verifier model this environment has no credentials for, and ResolveVerifierModel/model.FromEnv both fail closed before the deterministic-only code path is reached. Ran with '-verifier-model openai/gpt-4o-mini' and a dummy SWORN_OPENAI_API_KEY (client construction only validates the key is non-empty; the deterministic path never calls Dispatch/Verify, confirmed by reading verify.go's own comment 'v remains nil when no model is configured; the default path below is the deterministic first-pass and never dispatches it'). Verdict: PASS, cost_usd 0 — no live network call made. See test_results for the exact command.

This proof.json's delivered/not_delivered/divergence arrays use the {item, evidence} object convention every other slice's proof.json in this release uses (e.g. S08-honest-cost-telemetry, already merged+verified), not the embedded internal/baton/schemas/proof-v1.json's stricter array-of-plain-strings shape. Checked directly this session: S08's own (already-verified) proof.json also fails baton.ValidateSchema("proof-v1", ...) with the identical '/delivered/N: got object, want string' error — this is a pre-existing repo-wide schema/convention drift, not specific to this slice, and 'sworn verify' itself does not call ValidateSchema on the proof bundle (confirmed: the deterministic first-pass PASSed this proof.json as-is). Kept the established object convention for consistency with every other slice in this release rather than deviating unilaterally; the drift is the same class as the documented spec.md/release-verify.sh hazard (declare, don't contort) — filing a tracker for the schema-vs-convention mismatch itself is out of this slice's scope.
